evidence_sentence: 35S::MIR156A: LD flowering: 35.4 +/- 0.3 days vs Col 27.8 +/- 0.3 days (+7.6 days delay); juvenile leaves 26.4 +/- 0.5 vs Col 5.0 +/- 0.2 (+21.4 leaves)

Inflorescence height was measured between the rosette and the first flower; double mutant showed significantly reduced height (6.9 cm vs 12.1 cm in wild-type); double mutant produced approximately six extra rosette leaves compared to wild-type (19.5 vs 13.1 leaves); bolted and flowered somewhat later than wild-type
spl2/9/11/13/15 in LD: days to flower 33.0 +/- 0.3 vs Col 27.8 +/- 0.3 (+5.2 days); LFY, AP1, FUL, SOC1 transcripts all reduced approximately 50%
pSPL9::rSPL9 accelerated the expression of all adult-specific leaf traits, producing leaves with an elongated leaf blade, serrated leaf margin, and abaxial trichomes; increased miR172 levels >4-fold

toe1-2 toe2-1 double mutant: 6.0 rosette leaves vs wild-type 11.4 (significantly earlier than single mutants); The early-flowering phenotype of toe1-2 toe2-1 is consistent with a model in which miR172 causes early flowering by downregulating several AP2-like floral repressors
35S-EAT (miR172 OE): 2.0 rosette leaves at flowering; EAT-D flowers extremely early with 3.1 rosette leaves vs wild-type 11.4; miR172 overexpression caused early flowering phenotype
late flowering fca-1 mutant plants show delayed floral transition compared to wild-type; FCA functions in parallel to the vernalization pathway to down-regulate RNA levels of the floral repressor FLC
fpa-2 mutant: 17.8 +/- 1.4 rosette leaves in long days vs wild-type 7.2 +/- 0.6; mutations in FPA result in extremely delayed flowering

FLD contains an amine oxidase domain and is a homolog of the human H3K4me demethylase LSD1; FLD demethylates H3K4me2 at the FLC gene body; most mutants for above genes are recessive and showed late-flowering phenotype under both long- and short-day conditions

In the fve mutants, the FLC chromatin was enriched in acetylated histones H3 and H4, indicating FVE is required for histone deacetylation at FLC chromatin; late-flowering phenotype of acg1 was complemented by FVE overexpression
fy-5 caused a 90% increase in FLC transcript and delayed flowering
Transgenic 35S-TOE1 plants flowered much later than wild-type plants with average 28.6 rosette leaves; toe1-1D activation-tagged line showed late flowering with 22.5 rosette leaves vs 11.4 wild-type
smz-D (LD): 45.1 +/- 1.7 leaves vs WT 15.5 +/- 0.6; 35S::rSMZ plants remained vegetative throughout their life
toe1 toe2 smz snz quadruple mutant: 7.7 +/- 0.7 leaves, flowers significantly earlier (p<0.001), confirming redundant repression of flowering by AP2-like genes

Exogenous sucrose significantly accelerates abaxial trichome production in wild-type plants; sugar treatment reduced pri-MIR156A and pri-MIR156C abundance and mature miR156 levels, while SPL9 and SPL15 transcripts were markedly increased
ldl1 mutants flowered later than wild-type Columbia (Col) in long days; FLC was upregulated in ldl1 and ldl1 ldl2 mutants
ldl1-2 ldl2 double mutant flowered later than ldl1 single mutant; FLD, LDL1, and LDL2 act in partial redundancy to repress FLC expression
The presence of the FCA-gamma transgene overcomes FRI epistasis and plants flower earlier (21.8 +/- 0.3 leaves in FRI control vs 13.5 +/- 0.3 leaves with transgene)

mir172abcde plants flowered much later than Col-0; mir172abd and mir172abcde plants initially flowering from axillary shoots when grown under short photoperiods (approximately 91% of 77 plants); FT mRNA levels were between 27% and 31% of Col-0 levels in mir172abcde quintuple mutants

spl9/13/15 in SD: 31.5 +/- 0.8 juvenile leaves vs Col 7.0 +/- 0.2; 59.8 +/- 1.1 rosette leaves vs Col 42.7 +/- 0.7; spl9/13/15 produced 16-24 more juvenile leaves and 6-17 more rosette leaves than Col
ga1, ga2 and ga3 single mutants (defective in CPS, KS and KO, respectively) are extreme dwarfs with much reduced hypocotyl and root lengths and small rosette leaves
ga1, ga2 and ga3 single mutants ... are extreme dwarfs
ga3 single mutant ... is extreme dwarf
GA20ox1 plays a more prominent role in elongation of internodes and stamen filaments
35S::GA20ox1 transgenic Arabidopsis shows increased plant height and shoot fresh weight
GA3ox1 is expressed throughout all stages of plant development
the double homozygous mutant ga3ox1 ga3ox2 fail to germinate in the dark
GA2ox overexpression lines show late flowering and dwarf phenotypes
The triple mutant gid1a gid1b gid1c in Arabidopsis is a non-germinating extreme dwarf, whose phenotype is even more severe than the GA-deficient ga1-3 mutant
gai-1 semidominant mutation causes severe GA-insensitive dwarf phenotype with short stem
rga loss-of-function rescues stem elongation of ga1-3; single rga mutant in WT background has slightly taller bolt
Removing both RGA and GAI functions in the ga1-3 background completely rescues vegetative growth and floral initiation
Exogenous GA3 application to ga1-3 mutants rescues stem elongation and floral initiation
Removing both RGA and GAI functions in the ga1-3 background completely rescues vegetative growth and floral initiation; the quintuple della mutant is constitutively GA-responsive
Exogenous GA3 promotes stem elongation in WT plants
Loss-of-function spy mutations partially rescue all defects resulting from GA deficiency
sly1 mutant accumulates DELLA proteins and shows dwarfism
bri1 mutants show compact rosette, short petioles, short inflorescences
bin2-1 dominant gain-of-function is dwarf with compact rosette, phenocopying bri1 loss-of-function
bin2 loss-of-function mutants or triple bin2 bil1 bil2 mutants show elongated hypocotyls and enhanced BR responses
bzr1-1D gain-of-function mutation causes constitutive BR response and taller plants
bes1-D is a dominant gain-of-function mutation causing constitutive BR response with elongated stems
dwf4 is a BR biosynthetic mutant blocked in cell elongation; compact rosette and short stem
Overexpression of DWF4 was noted to increase inflorescence height
det2 mutants show dwarfism, short hypocotyl, and de-etiolated phenotype; rescued by brassinolide
cpd mutant is BR-deficient dwarf with compact rosette and short stem
Exogenous brassinolide (BL) promotes cell elongation and increases stem height in wild-type Arabidopsis
Brassinazole (BRZ) inhibits BR biosynthesis and phenocopies BR-deficient dwarf mutants
pif4 mutants fail to elongate hypocotyls at warm temperature
pif4 pif5 double mutants have reduced hypocotyl growth responses to low R:FR
pif1 pif3 pif4 pif5 (pifq) quadruple mutants have short hypocotyls and constitutive photomorphogenic phenotype in darkness
35S::PIF4 plants have elongated hypocotyls and partially rescue ga1 and gai-1 dwarf phenotypes
phyB mutants have elongated hypocotyls, petioles, and inflorescence stems
co mutants flower late in long days with more rosette leaves and larger rosette
35S::CO causes early flowering with few rosette leaves
gi mutants flower late in long days
fkf1 mutants flower late in long days due to failure to degrade CDF repressors
ztl loss-of-function stabilizes CO and slightly accelerates flowering
CDF1 overexpression represses CO and delays flowering
ft-1 mutants flower late in long days with additional rosette leaves
35S::FT causes extreme early flowering with very few leaves
fd mutants flower late and cannot respond to FT
soc1 mutants flower late with more rosette leaves
lfy mutants show delayed flowering and produce shoots instead of flowers at early positions
35S::LFY plants flower early with dramatically reduced biomass
ap1 mutants show delayed floral commitment and additional lateral shoots
svp-41 mutants flower early with reduced leaf number
flm mutants flower early
FRI-containing lines flower very late with very high FLC levels and large rosettes
flc null mutants flower early with few rosette leaves
vin3 mutants fail to down-regulate FLC after vernalization and remain late-flowering even after cold
vrn2 mutants fail to maintain FLC silencing after vernalization and reactivate FLC
Vernalization (prolonged cold) silences FLC and enables flowering in FRI FLC late-flowering accessions
Plants grown at 27 degrees C have elongated hypocotyls and petioles compared to 22 degrees C
arp6 mutants phenocopy plants grown at high ambient temperature with elongated hypocotyls
YUC8 overexpression increases free IAA levels and causes hypocotyl and stem elongation
arf6 arf7 arf8 triple mutant fails to respond to low R:FR treatment and has reduced elongation
Auxin treatment promotes hypocotyl and stem elongation by activating cell wall modification
hy5 mutants have elongated hypocotyls due to de-repression of growth genes
cop1 mutants show constitutive photomorphogenesis with very short hypocotyls
Conditional tps1 mutants (induced by dexamethasone) show extreme delay in flowering and accumulate vegetative biomass
tem1 tem2 double mutants flower very early with de-repressed FT and GA3ox
TEM1 overexpression delays flowering by repressing FT and GA biosynthesis
bzr1 bes1 multiple mutants are dwarf with compact rosette
kib1 mutants accumulate active BIN2 and show BR-insensitive dwarf phenotype
BKI1 overexpression blocks BR signaling and causes dwarf phenotype
pif4 mutants fail to elongate at 27 degrees C (pif4 blocks the warm-temperature elongation response)
phyB mutants show constitutive shade avoidance with elongated hypocotyls even in high R:FR
bri1 mutants cannot be rescued by exogenous BL because perception is abolished
Exogenous brassinolide restores WT phenotype in det2 mutants
gai-1 is GA-insensitive; exogenous GA fails to rescue gai-1 dwarfism
cry1 cry2 double mutants flower late in long days and show elongated hypocotyls
phyA mutants flower slightly later in long days
FCA-gamma transgene overcomes FRI epistasis and causes earlier flowering
CCA1 overexpression disrupts circadian rhythm and delays flowering
max2 mutants accumulate BES1 and show altered shoot branching
Exogenous GA3 rescues the dwarf phenotype of ga1-3 to near-WT height
ipt3 ipt5 ipt7 triple mutants show CK-deficient phenotype with reduced shoot growth
CKX overexpression reduces cytokinin levels and produces dwarfed plants with smaller rosettes
The ahk2 ahk3 double mutants exhibited a semidwarf phenotype with reduced leaf size and reduced inflorescence stem length
the ahk2 ahk3 ahk4 triple mutant showed markedly inhibited growth and development in various tissues
Multiple type-B ARR mutants affect cytokinin responses including inhibition of root elongation, lateral root formation, and hypocotyl elongation
High exogenous cytokinin inhibits primary stem elongation and hypocotyl growth
ctr1 mutants exhibit constitutive ethylene response with compact rosette and short stem in light
ein2 mutants are ethylene-insensitive with slightly longer stems under normal conditions
ein3 mutants are ethylene-insensitive
etr1 mutants are ethylene-insensitive with marginally increased elongation
ACC or ethylene gas treatment causes triple response in the dark and reduces stem elongation in light
ebf1 ebf2 double mutants accumulate EIN3 and show constitutive ethylene phenotype with severe dwarfism
max1 mutants are SL-deficient with increased branching and shorter main stem
max3-9 shows SL-deficient bushy phenotype with more branches and reduced main stem height
max4 SL-deficient mutants show increased branching and reduced main stem elongation
d14-1 mutant cannot perceive SL and shows increased branching with shorter main stem
The smxl6 smxl7 smxl8 triple mutant completely restored max3-9 and displayed a significant decrease in secondary cauline branch number
GR24 treatment rescues max3/max4 branching phenotype and restores main stem dominance
ABA application inhibits plant growth including stem elongation
ABI5 overexpression reduces seedling growth including hypocotyl and stem elongation
abi1-1 dominant mutant is ABA-insensitive with reduced ABA growth inhibition
pyr1 pyl1 pyl2 pyl4 quadruple mutants are ABA-insensitive in stomatal closure and growth inhibition

elf4 mutants have elongated hypocotyls
lux mutants are early flowering with elongated hypocotyls
bp mutants display compact inflorescence with short pedicels and internodes
stm loss-of-function mutants fail to maintain shoot apical meristem and cannot form normal shoots
hypocotyl elongation of hfr1 mutants is more significantly promoted by low R:FR
transgenic seedlings overexpressing HFR1 display suppressed hypocotyl elongation
Overexpression of PAR1 or PAR2 reduces the hypocotyl-growth response to low R:FR
the pin1 mutant in Arabidopsis is characterized by a stem nearly devoid of organs; pin1 mutants showed reduced PAT

The morphological phenotype of pin1 could be phenocopied by treatment with PAT inhibitors
